title: Estonian Border Guard patrol boat sinks in Mediterranean
content: Five people were injured when an Estonian patrol boat involved in a European Union Border Agency mission sank in the Mediterranean on the 16th of March

id: The accident occurred at around 4:20 p.m. local time
title: near the Greek island of Megisti near Turkey
content: and there were three Estonian border guards
url: a representative of the local border guard and the Estonian ambassador to Greece on board at the time.

title: Director General of the Estonian Police and Border Guard Board (PPA)
content: said that the accident was serious and that the circumstances of the incident were currently being investigated. He noted that it is known that the boat capsized and the people got out of it on their own.

id: Four people were taken to hospital by helicopter and are receiving medical treatment.
The condition of two people is assessed as serious.
Estonian Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna thanked the rescuers and the Greek Coast Guard for their quick and professional action. The minister added that Estonian authorities
title: in cooperation with their Greek counterparts
content: would investigate the cause of the accident.

id: The Estonian team has been participating in the European border guard Frontex mission since 2016. Greek public broadcaster ERT reported that the boat may have hit a dry dock while leaving or entering the port
title: and then started taking on water.

Во время войны свободы и права человека ограничиваются всегда и везде — такова суровая реальность. Неужели в ООН этого не понимают? Как можно предъявлять требования мирного времени стране

title: Russian disinformation campaigns targeting Estonia 2026
content: Russia is conducting a coordinated disinformation campaign targeting Estonia in 2026
url: with the most significant effort being a propaganda push promoting the creation of a "Narva People's Republic" in the Russian-speaking border city of Narva.[1][5]

title: Russian-linked social media accounts have spread content across Telegram
content: VK
url: and TikTok calling for the separation of Narva and the Ida-Viru County region from Estonia.[4] The campaign employs memes
channel: and internet imagery to normalize separatist rhetoric.[4] Posts have called for leaflet distribution
views: and armed mobilization under slogans such as "Russians
cluster_id: we are not alone!" and "From Narva to Püssi stretches Russian land."[1][5] The materials include maps and flags depicting the alleged "republic."[1]

id: Ukrainian and Estonian intelligence officials have identified striking similarities to Russian tactics preceding the 2014 annexation of Crimea and the creation of breakaway entities in Ukraine.[1][5] According to Andriy Kovalenko
title: head of Ukraine's Center for Countering Disinformation
content: this campaign mirrors the early stages of Russia's aggression against Ukraine more than a decade ago.[1] Before the formation of the "DPR" and "LPR" in Ukraine
url: similar social media communities promoting "people's republics" emerged as informational foundations for subsequent Russian occupation.[4]

id: Estonian security services view this campaign as a **potential precursor to destabilization efforts** and possible military invasion.[1][5] Marta Tuule
title: spokesperson for Estonian security service Kapo
content: characterized the posts as a disinformation campaign designed to create confusion and fracture social cohesion.[5] Intelligence sources noted the campaign's timing coincides with global attention focused elsewhere

id: Estonia has faced Russian influence operations for decades
title: including the 2007 DDoS attacks combined with social media radicalization efforts.[2] Russia continues employing propaganda centered on historical grievances
content: particularly World War 2 narratives
url: to influence Estonian society and build a "fifth column" of Russia-loyal residents.[3] The Kremlin systematically spreads disinformation about alleged anti-Russian discrimination and interethnic tensions to erode public trust.[3]
